Investing.com -- Gold prices are on track for their strongest monthly performance since the 1980s, underpinned by heavy investment demand, central bank buying and rising geopolitical and policy uncertainty. UBS raised its XAU/USD price target to $6200 per ounce for March, June and September 2026, up from $5000. UBS expects prices to ease modestly to $5,900 by the end of 2026, following the U.S. midterm elections. Investment activity was the main driver, with exchange-traded fund holdings rising by 801 metric tons and bar and coin purchases climbing to a 12-year high of nearly 1375 metric tons. Title: DXY, the US dollar index
# DXY, the US dollar index. The US dollar index (USDX, DXY, DX) is an index (or measure) of the value of the US dollar against a basket of foreign currencies, which is a basket of the currencies of US trading partners. The index rises when the US dollar gains *‘strength’* (value) against other currencies. DXY is a weighted geometric mean of the value of the dollar against the following selected currencies:. Initially, the value of the US dollar index was 100,000. The US dollar index was given a base value of 100,000 when it started. This means that a value of 90,000 represents a -10% decrease in the value of the dollar relative to the currencies in the basket (90,000 – 100,000), while a value of 110,000 represents a 10% increase in value (110,000 – 100,000).

results.title: US Dollar Index (DXY) Today
results.snippet: # US Dollar Index (DXY). The US Dollar Index (DXY) measures the value of the US dollar relative to a basket of six major foreign currencies: Euro (57.6% weight), Japanese Yen (13.6%), British Pound (11.9%), Canadian Dollar (9.1%), Swedish Krona (4.2%), and Swiss Franc (3.6%). The DXY is critical for emerging markets like India because a strengthening dollar (rising DXY) typically leads to capital outflows from emerging markets, commodity price declines (as most commodities are USD-denominated), and currency depreciation. Traders watch the DXY as a barometer of global risk appetite and USD strength. If you had invested in US Dollar Index (DXY):. results.title: 10-Year Treasury Bond Yield: What It Is and Why It Matters
results.snippet: # 10-Year Treasury Bond Yield: What It Is and Why It Matters. These rates indicate the confidence of investors in the economy. * The 10-year yield is used as a proxy for mortgage rates and is also seen as a sign of investor sentiment about the economy. The 10-year Treasury note yield is commonly used as a measure of the U.S. government’s 10-year borrowing rate. It serves as a benchmark for other interest rates and is a key indicator of investor sentiment about economic conditions. ## Understanding 10-Year Treasury Bond Yields. The U.S Treasury sells bonds via auction, and yields are set through a bidding process.3 Prices for the 10-year bond drop when confidence is high, which causes yields to rise. Best money market accounts. # The 10-year Treasury yield is moving the 'wrong way' for stocks. **What happened:** The 10-year yield (^TNX) rose about 6 basis points to 4.68% Tuesday, while the 30-year yield (^TYX) rose 5 basis points to 5.2%, hitting its highest level since July 2007. As Yahoo Finance's Jared Blikre has written, yields on both the 10-year and 30-year Treasurys are testing key psychological levels for investors, who are weighing the impact of inflation from the surge in oil prices on global growth.
